div.h1: What this is, and where it sits

g.title: Untrusted. Anything in here is assumed reachable by an attacker.
Trusted control plane. The egress edge exchanges the session JWT for the real credential, which lives only in the broker.

ul.li: 🔴 red = untrusted guest — where this binary runs
🔵 blue = trusted host — where the broker and the one credential live
🟢 green = the real storage, reached only by the broker
The guest dials one outbound HTTPS connection to the edge, and it never holds a backend key.

g.title: Holds NO backend credential, NO object-store client. Only a filesystem_id and an edge-only session JWT.
Validates the session JWT, strips it, exchanges it (RFC 8693) for the real storage credential keyed on filesystem_id, and injects it before the filestore.
Custodies the backend credential, signs every backend request, decides authorization per request.

text.tspan: TLS to the edge CA · static Bearer JWT
(the ONLY way out — no backend credential crosses)
JWT exchanged for the real credential

g.title: Untrusted. Anything in here is assumed reachable by an attacker.
Trusted control plane. The egress edge exchanges the session JWT for the real credential, which lives only in the broker.
